mechanism_narrative: A high level of the unemployment rate reflects deficient aggregate demand and pressures corporate earnings downward, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of months when UNRATE exceeds 7%, or Rank IC is greater than -0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned below 0.25%, the zero lower bound forces unconventional monetary accommodation that suppresses risk-free rates and drives investors into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, or the mean excess return in this regime is not statistically positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous combination of accommodative monetary policy indicated by a near-zero federal funds rate and elevated unemployment above 7% creates a disequilibrium that is resolved through asset-price inflation, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and UNRATE produces a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or the joint condition fails to outperform either single-proxy signal by at least 50% in hit rate.

mechanism_narrative: During early-cycle recoveries, accelerating retail sales signal reviving consumer demand and inventory restocking that lifts corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; this link weakens outside the recovery phase.
failure_condition: In quarters identified as early-cycle recovery, RSAFS fails to generate positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or the regime-conditional Sharpe ratio is below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production relative to recent trend signals an emerging supply-side earnings squeeze as capacity utilization falls and unit costs rise, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions coincide with positive S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the Rank IC between INDPRO changes and 5-day returns is greater than -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200 thousand persons signals robust labor income growth that feeds directly into consumer spending and top-line corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS rises by more than 200k in a month, S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the conditional mean return is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in consumer price inflation raises input cost pressures and prompts expectations of future monetary tightening, which reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates and lower real earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive correlation between CPIAUCSL acceleration and 20-day S&P 500 returns persists with Rank IC above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 900 thousand units indicate a frozen residential investment channel and diminished construction employment, which drags aggregate demand lower and generates negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of months when HOUST is below 900, or the conditional downside capture ratio is less than 1.0.
